document.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Adult Social Care Accelerator Guide

root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Adult Social Care Accelerator Guide
root.summary: Front matter and full guide for the Cúram 8.1.2 Adult Social Care accelerator. It states the guide applies to Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2, then outlines the document’s roadmap: accelerator overview and business scenario, installation/build prerequisites, XML server startup, a caseworker walkthrough, fit-gap and requirements analysis, and step-by-step application building. The accelerator is a worked social program example for incapacity benefits, using Case Management, Intake, Intelligent Evidence Gathering, Express Rules, and Workflow. The scenario covers a client moving from Sickness Benefit to Invalidity Benefit after six months, with tasks such as application creation, evidence management, authorization, payment generation, and certificate extension. Later sections cover configuring user views, evidence, products, intake, person match, and workflows, including custom event/task handling. The end of the section contains legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

children.title: Note
children.summary: Introductory note advising readers to review the Notices section before using the product or this information. Includes Merative copyright notice (2012, 2024).

children.title: Edition
Edition — pages 5-14
children.summary: Edition and front matter for the Adult Social Care accelerator guide. Confirms the edition applies to Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2, with Merative copyright/trademark notices. The section also previews the document contents and the main tutorial flow: overview of the Adult Social Care accelerator, installation prerequisites and build steps, XML server startup, caseworker scenario, analysis of requirements, building the application, and notices/privacy/trademarks.

children.title: Edition — pages 15-24
children.summary: Adult Social Care accelerator walkthrough for pages 15–24. Covers creating and submitting an application for James Smith, with detailed field values for claimant info, practitioner certificate, and diagnoses, then authorizing the application, activating the product delivery case, and generating a payment. Also includes a workflow workaround using an override server date, completing a task to extend a practitioner certificate, and change-in-circumstance scenarios: switching Incapacity Benefit Product Type and adding a new practitioner certificate/diagnosis via dynamic evidence screens or the Evidence Management wizard, with resulting determination/payment impacts.

children.title: Edition — pages 25-34
children.summary: This section covers Adult Social Care fit-gap analysis and configuration assessment. It shows how to compare customer requirements against default Cúram functionality using product docs and CADT, with examples around practitioner certificate evidence and person phone details, identifying gaps like missing inpatient indicator, facility name, label mismatches, and code table impacts. It then outlines organization and location structure design, location-based security (LBS), security IDs, user roles, and default user application views, especially administrator, system administrator, and eligibility worker.

children.title: Edition — pages 35-44
children.summary: This section explains how to configure Adult Social Care intake and application case processing: ownership strategy, program-level vs application-level authorization, eligibility checks, workflows, and evidence setup. It details correction-only vs succession evidence, evidence brokering, and verification architecture, including verifiable data items, verification requirements/usages, item utilizations, and dependent data items. It also notes Adult Social Care choices: eligibility checks are disabled, authorization is program level, and key evidence types like Practitioner Certificate and ASC Diagnosis are correction-only while Incapacity Benefit Product Type supports succession.

children.title: Edition — pages 45-54
children.summary: This section covers Adult Social Care accelerator setup and design guidance: conditional verifications, evidence brokering, case ownership strategy, and Evidence Management wizards. It also outlines product delivery requirements, eligibility determination defaults, and financial processing/payment generation, including delivery patterns and financial calendars. The latter pages provide build/configuration steps for the application: component order, user application views, home pages, personalized pod pages, and tabs. Useful for opening if you need implementation details or default ASC configuration values.

children.title: Edition — pages 55-64
children.summary: Cúram Adult Social Care accelerator setup instructions for pages 55–64. Covers configuring home/workspace sections and tabs, application banners and search settings, application code linking, user creation and assignment to an organization position, quick links, and saving user application view configurations. Includes specific Adult Social Care values such as ASCEWAPPHomeSection, ASCEWAPPWorkspaceSection, ASCEWAPP, ASCEligibilityWorkerHome, application ID ASCEWAPP, user ascewuser, and eligibility worker quick links.

children.title: Edition — pages 65-74
children.summary: This section is a configuration guide for the Adult Social Care accelerator, covering how to save rebuilt application artifacts under source control. It explains updating unique IDs in DMX/code tables, saving personalized pod/page configs, creating application view files (.app/.sec/.tab/.mnu/.ssp/.properties), saving application code in CT_APPLICATION_CODE.ctx, saving user and quick-link DMX files, and configuring evidence entities. It includes file locations, build targets, and notes on Adult Social Care-specific overrides such as ASCEligibilityWorkerHome and quick links.

children.title: Edition — pages 75-84
children.summary: This section covers Adult Social Care accelerator setup from extracting and saving configured dynamic evidence artifacts through configuring products, integrated cases, evidence types, and product delivery cases. It details required DMX, CLOB, BLOB, code table, and tab/nav files; batch extraction/build steps; and source control locations. It also defines Adult Social Care code tables and case settings, including the Social Care integrated case type, evidence types (Practitioner Certificate, ASC Diagnosis, Incapacity Benefit), evidence sharing, and product delivery case wizard fields.

children.title: Edition — pages 85-94
children.summary: Describes Adult Social Care product setup in Cúram: wizard-entered benefit, delivery pattern, provision, location, and eligibility determination values; configuration of decision display categories; and how to save/export product, integrated case, and product delivery DMX, CTX, XML, blob/clob files. Includes file locations, code tables, build steps, and specific rule sets/rule classes for Incapacity Benefit. Useful for navigating product wizard configuration, case decision categories, and source-control/export tasks.

children.title: Edition — pages 95-104
children.summary: This section covers Adult Social Care accelerator setup across pages 95–104: required DMX, XML, XSD, properties, and UIM files for product delivery patterns, decision display categories, and IEG intake scripts/data stores. It explains a demo-only property change, saving decision category configuration, and the Adult Social Care-specific file locations. It also configures evidence types, a basic program, and an internal application, including the key values for Adult Social Care, Incapacity Benefit product delivery, and intake mappings.

children.title: Edition — pages 105-114
children.summary: Pages 105–114 describe Adult Social Care accelerator configuration steps and file locations for intake setup. Topics include setting “register as prospect” to No, saving application case, evidence type, program, internal application, and program-mapping configuration files, plus the related DMX/XML files, build-database steps, and accelerator directories. It also explains evidence security authorization, mapping configuration files, and the basics for deciding when to use an Evidence Management wizard. Useful for finding setup procedures, file references, and customization points.

children.title: Edition — pages 115-124
children.summary: This section covers Adult Social Care accelerator implementation details for an Evidence Management wizard: UI/page design considerations, struct conventions, facade modeling, mandatory fields, build steps, and the generated server/client files. It also lists how to configure person match search criteria and save related DMX files, then begins workflow configuration with instructions for modeling an enactment struct in Rational Software Architect. Open this node if you need wizard implementation artifacts, file locations, person match setup, or workflow struct modeling steps.

children.title: Edition — pages 125-134
children.summary: Adult Social Care accelerator setup for Cúram workflow/model configuration. Covers creating the model/package/fragment, defining domain definition CORRECTION_SET_ID, creating struct CaseIDCorrectionSetID with caseID and correctionSetID attributes and code package ascproduct, then configuring workflow events (ASCCertExtension/ExtendCert, ASCTask/Closed), a manual workflow “ASC Extend Practitioner Certificate,” WDO and enactment mappings, a manual activity “ASC Extend Certificate Decision,” and an event wait tied to ASCTask CLOSED using correctionSetID.

children.title: Edition — pages 135-144
children.summary: This section explains how to configure and save an Adult Social Care workflow: task details, text parameters, actions, business object associations, allocation strategy, and transitions. It gives example values for the “ASC Extend Practitioner Certificate” workflow, including task subject and priority, a workspace action, Case business object association, and case owner allocation. It also covers editing/releasing workflows, exporting XML, creating EVX event files, and implementing workflow enactment and post-event logic to start a workflow or close a task when evidence changes.

Edition/front matter for the Adult Social Care accelerator guide. Confirms applicability to Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2 with Merative copyright/trademark notices, then previews the full guide: accelerator overview and business scenario, install/build prerequisites, XML server startup, caseworker walkthrough, fit-gap analysis, application build/configuration, evidence, products, intake, Evidence Management wizard, person match, and workflow setup. Open this node for the document’s scope and roadmap.
