children.summary: Defines Adult Social Care code package and workflow event configuration values. For the imported struct curam.ascproduct.struct.CaseIDCorrectionSetID, the code package value is ascproduct. Also lists workflow event setup steps and the required event class/type pairs: ASCCertExtension → ExtendCert and ASCTask → Closed. Useful if you need specific configuration values for Cúram Adult Social Care code generation or workflow event setup.

Explains how to set the code package property on an Adult Social Care struct so it can be imported and used from Eclipse. The procedure is: select the package containing the struct, open Properties, go to Advanced > curam.profile > Code_Package, enter the package structure, and save. This follows earlier struct setup for CaseIDCorrectionSetID and its attributes caseID and correctionSetID. Useful if you need the exact configuration step for making a Cúram struct accessible from Eclipse.

Explains how to configure a Cúram workflow enactment struct in Rational Software Architect. It covers creating a blank model under EJBServer\Models, creating the Workflow package and fragment files (Workflow.efx, DomainDefinitions.efx), defining the CORRECTION_SET_ID domain definition with type INTERNAL_ID, creating the CaseIDCorrectionSetID struct, adding attributes caseID (CASE_ID) and correctionSetID (CORRECTION_SET_ID), and setting the struct’s Code_Package property so it can be imported from Eclipse.

children.title: Configuring a workflow
Configuring a workflow event
Event values for Adult Social Care
children.summary: Explains how to configure workflow events in Cúram for Adult Social Care. It gives the steps to create an event class and event type in Administration Workspace, then lists the required values for two events: event class ASCCertExtension with event type ExtendCert, and event class ASCTask with event type Closed. Also includes nearby setup context for generating model changes and a code package value (ascproduct).

Explains how to configure a basic workflow event in Cúram. It gives the Administration Workspace steps to create a new event class and event type for manual and event-wait activities, then lists Adult Social Care values: event class ASCCertExtension with event type ExtendCert, and event class ASCTask with event type Closed. Also includes nearby setup context for generating model changes and the ascproduct code package value.

children.title: Configuring a workflow
Configuring a workflow process definition
Workflow values for Adult Social Care
children.summary: This section explains how to configure a Workflow process definition in the Administration Workspace and gives the exact values for an Adult Social Care manual workflow. It covers the meaning of process fields such as Name, Technical ID, Category, and split/join types, then specifies the manual workflow settings: Name “ASC Extend Practitioner Certificate,” Technical ID “ASCExtendPractitionerCertificate,” Category “Tasks,” and both Start Process Split Type and End Process Join Type set to None. It describes a workflow that creates a manual task assigned to the case owner.

Explains how to configure a Workflow process definition in the Administration Workspace by creating a new workflow process and understanding its fields: Name, Technical ID, Category, Start Process Split Type, End Process Join Type, and optional Description. For Adult Social Care, it gives the exact manual workflow values: Name “ASC Extend Practitioner Certificate,” Technical ID “ASCExtendPractitionerCertificate,” Category “Tasks,” with both split and join set to None. The workflow creates a manual task assigned to the case owner.

children.title: Configuring workflow data objects
Data objects values for Adult Social Care
children.summary: Defines the Adult Social Care workflow data object “CaseIDCorrectionSetID” and its attributes. The data object display name is “Case and correction set identifiers.” It contains two workflow data object attributes: CaseID and correctionSetID, both displayed as “Case identifier” and “Correction set identifier,” both 64-bit Integer Type, and both required for enactment. The section also explains that enactment mappings specify minimum required data for workflow execution and must be created for required attributes, including when using subflows or BPEL/process output scenarios.

Explains how to configure workflow data objects in a workflow process definition. Covers creating a WDO on the Data Objects tab, setting its name, display name, list flag, initialize flag, and description, then adding attributes with name, display name, and a valid Cúram base-domain type selected from DomainType. Notes that types must match mapped fields to avoid runtime failures.

children.title: Configuring the enactment mappings
Enactment values for Adult Social Care
children.summary: Defines Adult Social Care workflow enactment mappings for data object attributes. It specifies the required enactment values for CaseIDCorrectionSetID.caseID and CaseIDCorrectionSetID.correctionSetID, both 64-bit integers mapped to curam.ascproduct.struct.CaseIDCorrectionSetID. It also includes the workflow procedure for creating or refreshing enactment mappings in the Enactment tab and explains how to configure a manual activity, including required activity name uniqueness and optional category setup.

Explains how enactment mappings work for workflow data objects: if an attribute’s enactment flag is true, it must be mapped for the process to be enacted; false means it is not required. The Process Definition Tool creates mappings from WDOs marked required at enactment, and subflow parent processes must map all required subflow WDOs before release. For Adult Social Care, the CaseIDCorrectionSetID data object has two required 64-bit integer attributes: caseID and correctionSetID.

children.title: Configuring a manual activity
Configuring a manual activity
Manual activity values for Adult Social Care
children.summary: This section gives the configuration values for a manual workflow activity in Adult Social Care. It explains that manual activities require human intervention, can notify users of tasks, and assign work to agents. The example configuration is for “ASC Extend Certificate Decision” with Category “Standard,” Type “Manual,” Parallel Activity Type left blank, and Split/Join Type set to None. The activity’s purpose is to create a task for an eligibility worker to decide whether to extend a client’s certificate.

Explains how to configure a manual workflow activity. It defines an activity as a discrete unit of work that must be completed before the workflow can progress, and notes that activity names must be unique within a workflow process definition and are localizable. It also says activities can be optionally assigned to a category for search purposes, with categories taken from the CT_ActivityCategory.ctx code table.

children.title: Configuring an event wait
Event wait values for Adult Social Care
children.summary: Explains how to configure an event wait for a manual workflow activity in Cúram Adult Social Care. It describes assigning an event to notify the workflow engine when work is completed, with a brief step-by-step procedure using the Event Wait tab, event selection, and event match data selection. It also gives the specific Adult Social Care event wait values: Event Class ASCTask, Event Type CLOSED, and Event Match Data CaseIDCorrectionSetID.correctionSetID. The section then begins the next topic, configuring a workflow task.

Introduces event wait configuration in Adult Social Care workflow process definitions. It explains the activity type context alongside other workflow activity types, then gives the manual activity values used nearby: activity name “ASC Extend Certificate Decision,” category Standard, type Manual, with split/join set to None and no parallel type. This section serves as the lead-in to configuring an event wait for workflow completion notifications.

children.title: Configuring a workflow task
Task values for Adult Social Care
children.summary: Defines Adult Social Care task values and task action setup. The task should use subject text “Practitioner Certificate needs to be extended” with High priority. It then explains how to configure task actions for a manual activity: add actions from the Task Details tab, with fields for Page ID, Action Text, Principal Action, and Open In Modal. It notes actions must point to valid Cúram pages and match page parameters.

This section explains how to configure a workflow task for a manual activity. It covers editing Task Details, setting the task subject text with replaceable parameters, selecting priority, and configuring optional fields such as mapped priority, administration SID, manual forward, deadline override, and initial comment. It also introduces text parameter setup after saving. The section is useful if you need to define what users see in their inbox and how manual workflow tasks behave.

children.title: Configuring a task action
Action values for Adult Social Care
children.summary: Defines Adult Social Care action configuration values and how to set up a business object association for a workflow task. Key action values: Page ID `Evidence_workspaceActiveHighLevel`, Action Text `Evidence Workspace Home Page`, Principal Action `Yes`. Also notes the “Multiple Occurring Action” behavior, where one action record is created per list item and a link parameter must come from the list workflow data object. Business object associations link tasks to application entities; the data object attribute must map to a LONG identifier such as case or participant ID.

Explains how to configure task actions for a workflow activity in Cúram. Actions are links to valid application pages, and the page parameters must match the task action link parameters. The procedure is: open Task Details > Actions, create a new action, and set fields such as Page ID, Action Text, Principal Action, and optional Open In Modal. It also states that principal actions are the main work links, while secondary actions point to supporting information.

children.title: Configuring a business object association
Business object association values for Adult Social Care
children.summary: Explains how to configure a business object association for an Adult Social Care workflow activity. It defines the association as a link between a task and application entities like Case or Concern, and notes the workflow data object attribute must map to a valid business object type and be assignable to LONG (for a unique ID such as a case or participant identifier). Includes the basic procedure: open the Business Object Associations tab, create a new association, enter Business Object Type and Data Object Attribute, then save.

Explains how to configure a business object association for an Adult Social Care workflow activity. The section says these associations link a task to application entities such as Case or Concern, and are useful for manual activities/workflows acting on existing records. It gives the procedure: open the Business Object Associations tab, click New, then enter a Business Object Type from CT_BusinessObjectType.ctx and a Data Object Attribute. The mapped data object must be valid and assignable to LONG, since it represents a unique ID such as a case or participant identifier.

children.title: Configuring an allocation strategy
Allocation strategy values for Adult Social Care
children.summary: This section explains how to configure an allocation strategy in Cúram workflow for Adult Social Care. It defines the three supported allocation strategy types—Function, CER, and Target—and gives the specific Adult Social Care value: Function allocation using `curam.core.sl.intf.WorkflowAllocationFunction.caseOwnerAllocationS`. It also briefly introduces transition configuration, describing transitions as unidirectional links between activities in a process definition. Useful if you need workflow allocation setup or a high-level overview of transitions.

Explains how to configure a workflow allocation strategy for manual activities in Cúram. It defines the three supported types—Function, CER, and Target—and gives the Adult Social Care function allocation value `curam.core.sl.intf.WorkflowAllocationFunction.caseOwnerAllocationS`. Also introduces transition setup, noting that transitions are unidirectional links between activities that control execution order, with at least one start and one end activity in every process definition.

Explains how to configure an Adult Social Care manual workflow activity and its related setup. Covers creating the activity, unique/localizable activity names, optional category (CT_ActivityCategory.ctx), manual type with split/join set to None, and the example activity “ASC Extend Certificate Decision.” Also describes configuring the associated event wait, task details (subject text parameters, priority, administration SID, forwarding, deadline override, initial comment), task actions (page links, principal actions, modal), and business object associations for linking tasks to entities like Case or Concern.

children.title: Configuring a transition
Transition values for Adult Social Care
children.summary: This section defines Adult Social Care workflow transition values and briefly explains workflow editing/releasing. It specifies the transition configuration from Start Process Activity to ASC Extend Certificate Decision, and from ASC Extend Certificate Decision to End Process Activity. It also includes steps for editing an existing released workflow and releasing an in-edit workflow. The configured Adult Social Care workflow is named ASC Extend Practitioner Certificate and belongs to the Tasks category.

Explains how to configure workflow transitions in Cúram. Defines transitions as unidirectional links that control activity order, allowing multiple incoming and outgoing transitions per activity. Notes that every process definition must have exactly one start and one end activity. Useful for understanding workflow flow control and whether to open the section for transition setup details.

This section explains how to configure an Adult Social Care workflow in Cúram: create the process definition, define a WDO with required 64-bit integer attributes, add enactment mappings, and set up a manual activity that creates a task for the case owner. It also covers event wait, task subject/priority, action links, business object associations, allocation strategy, and transitions. The Adult Social Care example uses “ASC Extend Practitioner Certificate” and “ASC Extend Certificate Decision,” with manual split/join set to None and a task action to the Evidence Workspace home page.

children.title: Editing an existing workflow
Workflow created for Adult Social Care
children.summary: This section shows the Adult Social Care workflow configuration. It describes creating transitions between activities, including the source and target activity IDs, and gives the specific transition values for the ASC Extend Practitioner Certificate workflow. It also explains how to edit an existing released workflow and how to release an in-edit workflow. Useful if you need workflow setup details, transition configuration, or release/edit procedures for Adult Social Care.

Explains how to edit an existing released workflow in Administration Workspace: go to Workflow > Released Processes, locate the workflow, choose Edit > Save, then open it from Workflow > In-Edit Processes. Also notes how to release an in-edit workflow from the Home tab. Includes Adult Social Care workflow configuration details and transition setup context, with example transition values for ASC Extend Practitioner Certificate. Useful for workflow edit/release procedures and Adult Social Care setup.

children.title: Releasing a workflow
children.summary: Explains how to release an in-edit workflow from the workflow Home tab by clicking Release Processes. Also includes related workflow administration content: creating transitions via the Transitions tab (with From Activity/To Activity 64-bit identifiers), example Adult Social Care transition values, and steps for editing an existing released workflow through Administration Workspace > Workflow > Released Processes and In-Edit Processes.

Explains how to configure an Adult Social Care workflow in Cúram. Covers creating workflow events, a process definition, WDOs and enactment mappings, a manual activity with task subject/priority, action link, business object association, allocation strategy, and transitions. Includes example values such as ASCCertExtension/ExtendCert, ASCTask/Closed, workflow ASC Extend Practitioner Certificate, WDO CaseIDCorrectionSetID with 64-bit integer attributes, and task action Evidence_workspaceActiveHighLevel. Also notes how to edit and release workflows in Administration Workspace.

children.title: Saving your workflow configurations
Saving the released workflow configurations
Workflow file configurations for Adult Social Care
children.summary: Explains how to preserve workflow configurations for Adult Social Care before running a build database target. It instructs exporting workflow XML from the Workflow Home tab and saving it under the component’s workflow directory, specifically noting ASCExtendPractitionerCertificate_v1.xml in %CURAM_DIR%\EJBServer\components\AdultSocialCare\workflow. It also introduces creating event .evx files by combining event class and type data, with an example for ASCTask/Closed.

Explains how to preserve released workflow configurations before running a build database target. You export the workflow XML from the Workflow Home tab and save it in %CURAM_DIR%\EJBServer\components\%COMPONENT_NAME%\workflow, with Adult Social Care’s ASCExtendPractitionerCertificate_v1.xml shown as an example. It also covers creating event .evx files in the components\%COMPONENT_NAME%\events directory by combining event class and event type data, using ASCTask/Closed as the sample.

children.title: Creating an event file
Event configuration files for Adult Social Care
children.summary: Lists the Adult Social Care event configuration files and where they are stored. It notes that `%CURAM_DIR%` defaults to `C:\Merative\Curam\Development` and that building the EVX files is done from `%CURAM_DIR%\EJBServer` using the `build evgen database` target. The section covers `ASCTaskClosure.evx` (closes a task when Apply Changes is clicked) and `ASCTaskCertExtend.evx` (raises an event to extend certification and create a task for the case owner), both in `EJBServer\components\AdultSocialCare\events`.

Explains how to create an event EVX file for workflow/event configuration. It says event configurations extracted from the database appear in EVENTCLASS.dmx and EVENTTYPE.dmx, whose contents are added to an associated .evx file; if the event class and type are already known, extraction is unnecessary. The .evx file should be created under `%CURAM_DIR%\EJBServer\components\%COMPONENT_NAME%\events`, with an example `<events>` snippet defining an event class and event type.

Explains how to preserve workflow and event configurations before a build database target overwrites them. Workflow XML should be exported from the Workflow Home tab and saved under `%CURAM_DIR%\EJBServer\components\%COMPONENT_NAME%\workflow` (example: `ASCExtendPractitionerCertificate_v1.xml` for Adult Social Care). It also describes creating an event `.evx` file in `%CURAM_DIR%\EJBServer\components\%COMPONENT_NAME%\events`, using event class/type data from `EVENTCLASS.dmx` and `EVENTTYPE.dmx` or directly if already known, with an `ASCTask/Closed` example.

children.title: Implementing a workflow enactment
Implementing a workflow enactment
children.summary: Explains how to implement and export a workflow enactment struct to start a workflow in Adult Social Care. Covers importing the generated struct, then using custom code (extending `PaymentInstrumentEvent`) to detect expiring evidence/payment schedules, find the related case and active evidence, extract `correctionSetID`, and trigger `EnactmentService.startProcess("ExtendEvidenceWorkflow", ...)`. Notes the struct must be added twice because enactment arguments are consumed in sequence.

children.title: Implementing a post event
children.summary: Explains how to implement a post event class in Cúram 8.1.2 to run custom logic after evidence activation, such as closing a caseworker’s task when specific evidence is modified and Apply Changes is clicked. Shows extending `EvidenceActivationEvents`, overriding `postActivation(CaseKey, EvidenceList)`, checking for modified evidence with `SUPERSEDED` status, and raising a task-closure event using the evidence correction set ID.

Explains how to implement Adult Social Care workflow automation via custom events. The workflow enactment example extends `PaymentInstrumentEvent`, checks for expiring evidence/payment schedules, finds the related case and active evidence, extracts `correctionSetID`, and starts `ExtendEvidenceWorkflow` with an enactment struct passed twice because arguments are consumed in sequence. Also covers a post-activation event by extending `EvidenceActivationEvents` and overriding `postActivation` to detect modified evidence (`SUPERSEDED`) and raise a task-closure event using the correction set ID.

This section explains how to configure Cúram workflows in RSA and Administration Workspace. It covers creating a workflow enactment struct and domain definition, setting up packages/fragments, defining workflow events, a manual process, WDOs and transitions, and saving XML/EVX configs before build database targets overwrite them. It also shows how to implement custom enactment and post-activation code to start workflows or close tasks based on evidence/payment changes, using Adult Social Care examples such as ASC Extend Practitioner Certificate and CaseIDCorrectionSetID.

Guides how to build the Adult Social Care application end to end. Covers setting client/server component order via CustomEnvironment files, then configuring the user application view (home page, pods, tabs, sections, shortcuts), evidence, products, intake IEG files and process, person match, workflows, and related build/save steps. Includes Adult Social Care-specific IDs, file locations, and examples for creating workflow enactments and post-activation task handling. Useful as the main setup overview for developers implementing or reviewing the ASC accelerator.

Adult Social Care accelerator is a worked Cúram social program example showing the end-to-end application workflow for incapacity benefits. It covers the business scenario (Sickness Benefit transitioning to higher-rate Invalidity Benefit after six months), core components used (Case Management, Intake, Intelligent Evidence Gathering, Express Rules, Workflow), and the main caseworker requirements. It also includes installation/build steps, a full caseworker scenario, fit-gap analysis, and implementation guidance for building the app, including user views, evidence, products, intake, person match, workflows, and custom event/task handling.
